statement: The historical partial P1 run reflects genuine score-generation target exhaustion, but `getRunDiagnostics` overstates severity because it mixes failed attempts and telemetry/OCC side effects with terminal target failures.
rationale: Raw tables and `getRunSummary` show 300 failed score targets, while diagnostics show 700 `score_gen` request errors plus unrelated retry noise and successful artifacts on the same stage.
keyAssumptions: Missing score units correspond to exhausted score-generation targets rather than hidden apply-path corruption.
Diagnostics are intended as request-attempt forensics, not terminal target accounting.
validationCriteria: Missing units have exhausted `score_gen` targets and no `score_critic` target.
All rubric artifacts are present while score-unit failures remain.
Diagnostics error totals materially exceed failed-target totals for the same run.
critiquePoints: Some failed score-generation attempts cite OCC conflicts on `process_observability`, so pure parser failure is not the only error class.
The sampled run may not represent every historical P1 failure shape.
questions: Do telemetry OCC conflicts remain a material contributor after the recent engine hardening?
